ogTitle: Lower Hermitage vs Lower Light: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lower Hermitage and Lower Light.
heading: Lower Hermitage vs Lower Light — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lower Hermitage, South Australia and Lower Light, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lower Hermitage has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Lower Light (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lower Hermitage or Lower Light safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lower Hermitage has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Lower Light scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lower Hermitage scores 20/100 for bushfire risk. Lower Light scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lower Hermitage or Lower Light?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lower Hermitage has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.38% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
